title: leucanthemum gayanum subsp. antiatlanticum
intro: Leucanthemum gayanum subsp. antiatlanticum, also known as the Anti-Atlantic Leucanthemum or a variant of the Leucanthemum gayanum, is a plant species within the daisy family, characterized by its white or pink flowers and medicinal properties. The plant has been traditionally used for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties, which are known to aid in wound healing, reducing fever, and fighting off infections. Leucanthemum gayanum subsp. antiatlanticum is also believed to have various health benefits, including lowering cholesterol levels, reducing blood pressure, and exhibiting antimicrobial properties. In herbal preparations, the plant can be found in teas, infusions, and tinctures, often used to treat respiratory issues, gastrointestinal problems, and skin conditions.
